# `omit` Function

Remove specified keys from a map or object

Returns a new map or object with the keys listed in 'keys' removed. Keys that do not exist in the input are silently ignored.

<!-- signature generated by tfplugindocs -->
```text
omit(input dynamic, keys list of string) dynamic
```

<!-- arguments generated by tfplugindocs -->
1. `input` (Dynamic) The map or object to remove keys from.
1. `keys` (List of String) The keys to remove.
<!-- variadic argument generated by tfplugindocs -->


## Example Usage

### Basic usage

```terraform
locals {
  entry = { env = "prod", region = "europe-west1", service = { id = "api", port = 8080 } }
}

output "without_region" {
  value = provider::util::omit(local.entry, ["region"])
}
# Result: { env = "prod", service = { id = "api", port = 8080 } }
```

### Stripping internal metadata before passing to a resource

When the result of `combine` contains internal tracking attributes (like `id` in a nested service object), `omit` lets you strip them before using the value as resource configuration.

```terraform
locals {
  all = provider::util::combine([
    { key = "env",    items = ["dev", "prod"] },
    { key = "region", items = ["europe-west1", "us-central1"] },
  ])
}

resource "google_compute_network" "vpc" {
  for_each = local.all
  name     = "vpc-${each.value.env}-${each.value.region}"
  # Remove the 'env' and 'region' keys — pass only remaining attributes as labels
  labels   = provider::util::omit(each.value, ["env", "region"])
}
```
